01-welcome-home.notes: Eb std (½-step down) — in standard, play the shapes a ½-step 'sharp' of what sounds. RIFF-HEAVY. FAKE IT: down-tuned power chords tracking the main riff (Ebm-rooted), lock the marching 8th/16th hits and the big stop-stabs with the drums. CHORDS behind riff: Ebm–B–Gb–Db feel. FILLS: Eb min pent, 11th box. If it falls apart, drop to root power chords on the low string.

02-all-the-small-things.notes: Std, key C. CHORDS: C–G–F all the way (verse + chorus); bridge/na-na part F–G. FEEL: fast all-downstroke 8ths, tight stops, palm-mute optional. No fills needed — just stamina and clean cutoffs.

03-a-little-less-conversation.notes: Std, key Em. CHORDS: funky one-chord vamp on E7#9 (or Em) with quick pushes to C–D. FEEL: muted 16th-note funk, stay off the top — space is the groove. FILLS: E min pent open/12th, short bluesy stabs; keep them tiny if singing.

04-i-believe-in-a-thing-called-love.notes: Std (or Eb), key E. CHORDS: verse chug on E5; chorus A–B–E (throw in G#m). FEEL: driving glam 8ths, cocky. Leave the falsetto leads to whoever wants them. FILLS: C#m pent, 9th box.

05-thunderstruck.notes: Std, key B. Lead owns the intro tapping — don't fake it, just come in on the band. CHORDS: pound B5 under the verses; 'THUNDER' section is B5–A5–E5. FEEL: relentless 8th-note downstrokes (Malcolm role). FILLS: B min pent, 7th box.

06-don-t-stop-believin.notes: Std, key E. CHORDS: E–B–C#m–A loop (chorus resolves E–B–A). FEEL: steady quarter/8th chords or light arpeggios under the piano; grow into the chorus. Keys carry the intro riff. FILLS: C#m pent, 9th box.

07-tribute.notes: Std, acoustic feel. CHORDS: follow the vocal — quiet verses around Am/G, big rock payoff on G–C–D. FEEL: it's a storytelling dynamic song, quiet-to-loud is the whole trick. FILLS: A min pent, 5th box for the 'demon' section.

08-while-my-guitar-gently-weeps.notes: Std, key Am. CHORDS: verse descends Am–G–D/F#–F–E7; chorus 'I look at the world' A–C#m–F#m–Bm–E. FEEL: slow, let chords ring. FILLS: A min pent 5th box — bends, vibrato, and SPACE; 2–3 recurring licks beat noodling.

09-pride-and-joy.notes: Key E (Eb original, but standard backing is fine). CHORDS: 12-bar blues E7–A7–B7. FEEL: the Texas SHUFFLE is the actual gig — swung, in the pocket, thumb bass + stabs. Get the groove before any licks. FILLS: E min/maj pent, 12th box, sparse.

10-zombie.notes: Std, key Em. CHORDS: Em–C–G–D the ENTIRE song (never changes). FEEL: clean/quiet verse, same chords big + distorted on the chorus — dynamics do all the work. FILLS: Em pent, sparse. Easy money.

11-hunger-strike.notes: Std, key ~D. CHORDS: verse vamps G–D; chorus opens up adding A / Bm (big). FEEL: slow and heavy, clean-to-dirty texture — sit UNDER the two trading vocals. FILLS: Bm pent, 7th box. CONFIRM exact changes + who cues them.

12-s-o-b.notes: Std, key ~G (CONFIRM). CHORDS: bluesy vamp with percussive muted chord hits; turnaround pushes C–D. FEEL: stomp/clap pocket — you may barely play in the intro. Big repeated shout outro. Muted stabs > strumming.

13-save-a-horse-ride-a-cowboy.notes: Std, key G. CHORDS: G–C–D (verse chucks on G). FEEL: tight country-rock chord chucks right on the beat, party energy. FILLS: G major / E min pent. Know the party-song form so you land the stops.

14-sweet-home-alabama.notes: Std, key D. CHORDS: D–C–G the whole song (the signature riff is just those roots — optional). FEEL: relaxed Southern 8ths, let it breathe. FILLS: D MAJOR pentatonic = Bm-shape 7th box; classic turnaround licks between vocal lines.

15-dani-california.notes: Std, key Am. CHORDS: verse riff Am–G–Dm; chorus lifts to a brighter run (Bb–F–C–G feel) — CONFIRM. FEEL: punchy verse riff, open chorus strums. FILLS: A min pent, 5th box, Hendrix-ish bends. Solo can be loose if no one owns it.

16-teenage-dirtbag.notes: Std, key E. CHORDS: palm-muted verse riff E5–D5–A5; chorus E–B–A. FEEL: quiet muted verse, big open chorus — dynamics again. CONFIRM the verse riff shape with the band. FILLS: not the point here.

17-the-pretender.notes: DROP D. CHORDS: dead-quiet intro (clean arpeggios, Bm/A area) then EXPLODE into Drop-D one-finger power chords on the main riff. FEEL: the whole song is the quiet→slam dynamic; nail the big entrance hit with the drums. FILLS: A min pent, 5th box.

18-here-it-goes-again.notes: Std, key C/G. CHORDS: bright riff around C–G–F / Am; steady 8ths with tight stops. FEEL: clean pop-rock, precision + the stops sell it. CONFIRM if the live arrangement differs from the record.

19-purple-rain.notes: Std — MATCH the singer's key (original Bb; live often G or A). CHORDS: I–vi–V–IV. In Bb: Bb–Gm–F–Eb. In G: G–Em–D–C. FEEL: slow 6/8, big clean ringing chords, huge dynamics. FILLS: match-key pent, slow emotional bends — less is more.

20-when-i-come-around.notes: Std (Eb original). CHORDS: G–D–Em–C the entire song (I–V–vi–IV). FEEL: lazy but DEAD steady 8ths, light palm mute; power chords only is totally fine. FILLS: none needed — just don't rush.

21-the-middle.notes: Std, key D. CHORDS: intro riff on D; body D–A–Bm–G (I–V–vi–IV). FEEL: tight, upbeat 8th-note strumming, lift into the chorus. FILLS: skip — rhythm consistency is the whole job. CONFIRM if they want the exact intro lick.

22-dream-on.notes: Std, key Fm. CHORDS: verse descending arpeggios Fm–Eb–Db–C7; big build into the chorus (Ab/Eb area). FEEL: clean fingered arpeggios, let the drama build. FILLS: F min pent, 1st/13th box — save any lead for the outro screamer only.

23-lola-montez.notes: D standard (CONFIRM tuning). CHORDS: main groove/riff around Dm, chorus hook. FAKE IT: down-tuned power chords tracking the riff roots, steady heavy rhythm. FILLS: D min pent, 10th box. Learn the chorus hook so it's recognizable.

24-mary-jane-s-last-dance.notes: Std, key Am. CHORDS: Am–G–D loop most of the song (chorus can add A). FEEL: relaxed roots-rock, let chords ring, don't rush. FILLS: A min pent, 5th box — this is your best 'tasteful fills between vocals' song; think harmonica-style bends.

25-valerie.notes: Std — CONFIRM version/key (upbeat Zutons in E, or slower Winehouse-style). CHORDS: I–ii–IV vamp in verse, IV–V–I into the hook (E: E–F#m–A–B). FEEL: bouncy soul groove, clean chord stabs, don't overplay. FILLS: C min pent, 8th box per the app if they do the down-a-bit key.

26-voodoo-child-slight-return.notes: Eb (or E on a standard track). Lead/riff song — CONFIRM it's even in the set and who solos. CHORDS: wah riff on E7#9, blues moves E–A–G. FAKE IT: simple E/Eb blues riffing, control the wah/noise; don't chase full SRV/Hendrix. FILLS: Eb min pent, 11th box.

27-fat-bottomed-girls.notes: DROP D (CONFIRM). CHORDS: intro is acoustic-ish, then big chunky Drop-D chords; chorus in the D–A–G area. FEEL: heavy but bouncy, nail the big stops. FILLS: D major pent, Bm-shape 7th box. Ask which Queen guitar layer they want.

28-mama-i-m-coming-home.notes: Std, key D. CHORDS: verse D–G–A (add the Dsus moves); chorus 'Mama, I'm coming home' G–A–D. FEEL: clean/edge rhythm, ballad-to-rock lift. FILLS: C#m/E-major pent, 9th box — lyrical bends. Ask if they need the acoustic intro figure.

29-ain-t-goin-down-til-the-sun-comes-up.notes: Std, key G. CHORDS: fast G–C–D boom-chuck. FEEL: BREAKNECK tempo is the real challenge — simplify to clean on-beat chord chucks and DO NOT fall behind. FILLS: G/E min pent, but skip them if the tempo's eating you. CONFIRM if it's full speed.

30-pink-pony-club.notes: Std — CONFIRM key (rock jam tracks often G). CHORDS: pop loop I–V–vi–IV (in G: G–D–Em–C); clean verses, bigger chorus strums. FEEL: build to the chorus lift. FILLS: Em pent, 12th box; the recognizable solo is optional — ask.

31-banditos.notes: Std, key E/A country-rock. CHORDS: bar-band changes around E–A–B (or A–D–E if they lean A). FEEL: loose bar-band groove, open chords + twangy fills. FILLS: E or A min pent, short phrases. Find the root against the track first. CONFIRM solo vs rhythm.
